notes: Evaluates the post-fix generate-spec skill for task-output invariants. The fix made learner intake a build-time input (not a generation input) and mandated a single deterministic course-default per [slot]. Each eval runs the skill 3x independently per config to measure determinism (does the no-intake build target stay stable run-to-run?). Baseline = pre-fix skill snapshot (commit 799f3f6). Runs generate into isolated scratch-course copies so tracked spec.md is never mutated.

evals.name: langchain-determinism
evals.prompt: Generate the spec for this course. The course materials are in materials/notebooks/ and materials/transcripts/. Write spec.md.
evals.precedence_branch_expected: 1 (course has a clear toy example → the default should re-express the course example shape on a synthetic fixture corpus)
evals.expected_output: A self-contained spec.md that: never asks for/blocks on learner intake; emits learner-specific values as [slots] each with a course-derived default; names exactly ONE buildable target; and that target is the SAME across all 3 runs. No instruction to read materials/ at build time.

assertions.text: The run generated a spec autonomously without requesting learner intake or halting because intake was 'required/missing' (invariant 1).
Every learner-intake [slot] in the spec carries an explicit course-derived default; no slot is left as a bare unfilled placeholder (invariant 2).
The spec names exactly ONE buildable default target (a single project title + default project description), not an ambiguous 'course-example-or-agnostic' choice left to the builder (invariant 3a).
The single build target is the SAME across all 3 with_skill runs for this course — same project type/shape (invariant 3b, cross-run).
The spec contains no instruction to read course materials/ at build time; it is buildable from spec.md alone (invariant 4).

evals.name: mcp-determinism
evals.prompt: Generate the spec for this course. The course materials are in materials/notebooks/ and materials/transcripts/. Write spec.md.
evals.precedence_branch_expected: either branch acceptable, but must be consistent — tests the fallback path
evals.expected_output: A self-contained spec.md that: never asks for/blocks on learner intake; emits learner-specific values as [slots] each with a course-derived default; names exactly ONE buildable target; and that target is the SAME across all 3 runs. No instruction to read materials/ at build time.

assertions.text: The run generated a spec autonomously without requesting learner intake or halting because intake was 'required/missing' (invariant 1).
Every learner-intake [slot] in the spec carries an explicit course-derived default; no slot is left as a bare unfilled placeholder (invariant 2).

The single build target is the SAME across all 3 with_skill runs for this course — same project type/shape (invariant 3b, cross-run).
The spec contains no instruction to read course materials/ at build time; it is buildable from spec.md alone (invariant 4).
